quiz.title: Unit 1 Quiz 1: Scarcity and Choice
quiz.subtitle: Fundamental Economic Concepts, Opportunity Cost, and Decision Making
quiz.subject: Economics
quiz.topic: Scarcity, Choice, Opportunity Cost, Trade-offs, Factors of Production

questions.question: Scarcity exists because ________
questions.explanation: Scarcity arises because human wants are unlimited while resources are limited.

questions.question: Economics is primarily the study of ________
questions.explanation: Economics studies how society allocates scarce resources to satisfy unlimited wants.

questions.question: Opportunity cost is defined as ________
questions.explanation: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative given up when making a choice.

questions.question: A trade-off involves ________
questions.explanation: A trade-off means sacrificing one thing to gain another.

questions.question: Which is NOT a factor of production?
questions.explanation: Factors of production are land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship. Money is not a factor; it facilitates exchange.

questions.question: The production possibilities frontier (PPF) shows ________
options: Relationship between price and quantity supplied
Relationship between quantity supplied and quantity demanded
questions.explanation: The PPF shows the maximum combinations of two goods an economy can produce with available resources.

questions.question: A point inside the PPF indicates ________
questions.explanation: A point inside the PPF indicates underutilization of resources (unemployment or inefficiency).

questions.question: Economic growth shifts the PPF ________
questions.explanation: Economic growth (more resources or better technology) shifts the PPF outward to the right.

questions.question: According to economics, a rational decision maker chooses ________
questions.explanation: Rational choices maximize net benefit (total benefit minus total cost).

questions.question: Marginal analysis compares ________
questions.explanation: Marginal analysis examines the additional benefits and additional costs of a decision.

questions.question: Microeconomics focuses on ________
questions.explanation: Microeconomics studies individual households, firms, and markets.

questions.question: Macroeconomics focuses on ________
questions.explanation: Macroeconomics studies aggregate variables like GDP, inflation, unemployment, and economic growth.

questions.question: Ceteris paribus means ________
questions.explanation: Ceteris paribus is Latin for 'all other things held constant.'

questions.question: Normative economic statements are ________
questions.explanation: Normative statements express opinions or value judgments (what 'should be').

questions.question: Positive economic statements are ________
questions.explanation: Positive statements are factual and can be tested against evidence.

questions.question: Specialization leads to ________
questions.explanation: Specialization allows workers to focus on tasks where they have a comparative advantage, increasing efficiency.

questions.question: Comparative advantage means producing at ________
questions.explanation: Comparative advantage is the ability to produce a good at a lower opportunity cost than another producer.

questions.question: Absolute advantage means producing with ________
questions.explanation: Absolute advantage is the ability to produce a good using fewer inputs (more efficiently).

questions.question: A sunk cost is ________
questions.explanation: A sunk cost has already been incurred and cannot be recovered; it should not affect future decisions.

questions.question: Free goods have ________
questions.explanation: Free goods (like air) have zero opportunity cost because they are not scarce.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! Strong grasp of scarcity and choice concepts.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review opportunity cost and PPF shifts.
scoreMessages.50: Solid effort. Focus on factors of production and comparative advantage.
scoreMessages.0: Keep studying. Scarcity means unlimited wants, limited resources.
